project.name: AI Embodiment
project.description: Interactive AI embodiment experience — abstract visual presence for a mobile-first personal assistant

overview: A mobile-first interactive AI experience where a personal assistant helps users organize their projects, thoughts, and time. The core design challenge is the embodiment — an abstract, non-figurative visual presence that implies intelligence and cognition without relying on a face or character. The embodiment must communicate agent state (listening, thinking, speaking, waiting) through subtle non-verbal cues — motion, color, scale, and position — so the interaction feels alive and intentional rather than disembodied. The experience supports equal voice and chat modes. Voice is the expected primary entry point, with chat serving as both an accessibility path and a channel for richer data exchange (photos, documents).
goals: Create an abstract visual embodiment that implies intelligence and thought — not a character, but a presence
Communicate agent state clearly through non-verbal visual cues (motion, color, size, position)
Design equal-weight voice and chat interaction modes within a single unified experience
Achieve a modern, minimal aesthetic that remains warm and inviting for daily personal use
Mobile-first design that feels natural on phone screens
Enticing enough that users want to engage — not sterile or clinical
nonGoals: Skeuomorphic or figurative design — no faces, avatars, or anthropomorphic features
Technical architecture or implementation planning (visual design only)
Production-ready code (comps are design artifacts, not shippable)
Designing the backend AI capabilities or conversation logic

scenarios.title: Voice Conversation — Morning Planning
steps: User opens the app and sees the embodiment in its idle/waiting state — calm, subtly alive
User taps a microphone action or speaks a wake phrase; embodiment shifts to listening state
User says "What do I have today?"
Embodiment transitions to thinking state — a visible shift in motion/energy
Embodiment transitions to speaking state as the agent responds with the day's agenda
Agent finishes speaking; embodiment returns to listening or waiting state

scenarios.title: Chat Mode — Sharing a Document
steps: User switches to chat mode (or starts in chat mode)
Embodiment remains visible but compact, anchoring the top of the conversation
User types a message or attaches a photo/document
Embodiment reacts subtly to incoming input — acknowledging receipt
Agent responds in text; embodiment shifts through thinking → responding states
Conversation thread scrolls naturally with the embodiment as a persistent presence

scenarios.title: State Awareness
steps: At any moment, the user can glance at the embodiment and know: is the agent listening? Processing? About to speak? Waiting?
State transitions are smooth and organic — no jarring switches
Subtle enough to read unconsciously (like reading body language) but clear enough to never leave the user confused

constraints: Mobile-first: Primary design target is phone screens (375–430pt width)
Dual interaction modes: Voice and chat must coexist as equal modes within the same UI
Accessibility: WCAG 2.2 AA compliance — embodiment states must be perceivable through means beyond color alone
State clarity: Users must always be able to determine agent state at a glance
Performance: Embodiment animations must feel smooth — design should consider what's achievable in real-time rendering on mobile
openQuestions: What abstract form language best implies intelligence? (fluid, particle, geometric, light-based, hybrid?)
How much screen real estate does the embodiment occupy in voice mode vs. chat mode?
Should the embodiment have a consistent shape that modulates, or should it transform more dramatically between states?
What color direction supports "modern yet enticing" for a personal assistant?
How does the transition between voice and chat modes work spatially?
Should the embodiment respond to ambient context (time of day, user mood) or only to direct interaction?

resolvedQuestions.question: Figurative or abstract?
resolvedQuestions.rationale: Avoids uncanny valley; implies intelligence without faking humanity

resolvedQuestions.question: Voice or chat primary?
resolvedQuestions.decision: Equal modes, voice as likely entry point
resolvedQuestions.rationale: Accessibility + richer data exchange via chat

resolvedQuestions.question: State communication method?
resolvedQuestions.decision: Non-verbal visual cues (motion, color, size, position)
resolvedQuestions.rationale: Mirrors how humans read body language — subtle but effective

risks.risk: Abstract form feels meaningless or decorative
risks.impact: Users ignore the embodiment, defeating its purpose
risks.mitigation: Ground form changes in clear state logic; user test for readability

risks.impact: Users can't tell what the agent is doing
risks.mitigation: Test with unfamiliar users; ensure at least 2 cue dimensions per state

risks.impact: Poor performance, battery drain, janky feel
risks.mitigation: Design with mobile rendering constraints; provide reduced-motion alternatives

risks.risk: Voice + chat mode switching feels disjointed
risks.mitigation: Design a unified layout that accommodates both rather than two separate screens

risks.impact: Users don't feel warmth or connection
risks.mitigation: Mood board to establish warmth cues; explore color temperature and organic motion

designScope.area: Embodiment form language and motion system
Agent state visualization (idle, listening, thinking, speaking)

cycleLog.outcome: Captured direction: abstract embodiment, dual-mode, state-driven motion, modern/warm tone
